refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability is a high-level KPI that depends on multiple factors including fault detection, fault tolerance, maintenance efficiency, and system reliability. Refining the FR into sub-FRs allows targeted design parameters to address each aspect.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure system uptime and downtime over the evaluation period; simulate fault scenarios and recovery times; verify maintenance procedures and system diagnostics.
sub_frs: FR1: Detect faults promptly to minimize downtime.
FR2: Enable rapid recovery and repair to restore availability.
FR3: Maintain system reliability to reduce fault occurrence.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement continuous health monitoring and diagnostics subsystem.
DP2: Design modular components for quick replacement and repair.
DP3: Use redundant critical subsystems to tolerate faults.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement continuous health monitoring and diagnostics subsystem.

potential_sources: Health monitoring subsystem interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates fault detection functionality, minimizing coupling with other DPs.

assumptions_risks: Risk of incomplete fault coverage if sensors fail.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating redundancy in health monitoring sensors to mitigate risk.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Realize health monitoring and diagnostics functions related to safety and availability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor data from Vehicle for health monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts on detected faults impacting availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates status and diagnostics with NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of health monitoring logic.
Physical component focused; health monitoring logic diffusion high if placed here.
Teleoperation focuses on control, not diagnostics; poor containment.
Best containment and minimal diffusion for health monitoring and diagnostics.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning health monitoring DP to Safety Services module centralizes fault detection and alerting, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential interface overload on SafetyAlert and Telemetry if multiple subsystems report faults.
Dependency on reliable network link for timely diagnostics communication.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define interface protocols and data filtering to manage alert volume and network load.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (health monitoring subsystem) directly addresses fault detection sub-FR and supports availability target.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk if diagnostics logic overlaps with Vehicle embedded systems.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services module provides strong containment for DP with well-defined interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data analysis and fault injection tests is feasible within Safety Services module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of fault detection DP to Safety Services IM with explicit interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface specifications to reduce alert noise.
Consider redundancy in sensor data paths to improve reliability.

decision.justification: The current decomposition and assignment are sound but require further refinement of sub-FRs and additional DPs to fully cover availability aspects such as recovery and reliability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Detect faults promptly to minimize downtime.
FR2: Enable rapid recovery and repair to restore availability.
FR3: Maintain system reliability to reduce fault occurrence.

revise.why: To decompose availability into more granular sub-FRs enabling targeted DP and IM assignments for recovery and reliability.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Safety Services module has capacity to handle diagnostics and alerting.
open_risks: Sensor failures leading to undetected faults.
